bserved.
Then how somebody is going to determine the time of
conception "exactly"?! It is impossible, and thus the method
is not practical.
The texts like Brihat Jataka, Leghu Jataka etc provide some
methods to calculate "the time of birth" based on the "time of
conception". Possibly they must have used it to verify the
accuracy of birth time and time of conception by cross
verifying both, and ensuring that they are related.

moon is present. When moon comes to th_e sign that much
away from the sign in which it is placed in dwadasamsa, (in

Secrets o(Birth Time Rectification
the 1Oth month from the day of conception) the child will be
born]
But all these become possible only when some body has
at least an approximate understanding about time of sexual
intercourse (that lead to conception). I don't think it is possible
in today's world where sex is given too much importance.
